title: collaea pascuorum
intro: Collaea pascuorum, also known as the "thorny groundsel," is a perennial herb native to Australia and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Collaea pascuorum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support digestive health, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Collaea pascuorum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
